title: Framley Parsonage
description: Framley Parsonage — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "Framley Parsonage" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: Barchester
memes.description: Barchester, the cathedral city of Barsetshire, the ecclesiastical and social hub around which Trollope's Chronicles revolve.

memes.label: Benefice
memes.description: An ecclesiastical office endowed with property and income, held by a clergyman of the Church of England

memes.label: Chaldicotes
memes.description: Chaldicotes, the country house of Mr. Sowerby, centre of political intrigue and the fast set that leads Mark Robarts into financial trouble.

memes.label: The Church of England
memes.description: The Church of England, the established national church, the institutional framework within which all the ecclesiastical dramas of Framley Parsonage unfold.

memes.label: Clergyman
memes.description: A minister of the Church of England — in Victorian society, a clergyman occupied a distinct social class between gentry and professional men

memes.label: Dr. Grantly
memes.description: Dr. Grantly, Archdeacon of Barchester, the formidable churchman whose pride, political instinct, and paternal concern make him a central figure in the Barsetshire ecclesiastical establishment.

memes.label: Dr. Thorne
memes.description: Dr. Thorne, a physician of Barsetshire and uncle to Mrs. Frank Gresham, whose medical authority and personal integrity make him an unexpected figure in the romantic calculations surrounding Miss Dunstable.

memes.label: The Parliamentary Election
memes.description: The parliamentary election that forms a major political subplot in Framley Parsonage, involving the Duke of Omnium's interest, Mr. Sowerby's machinations, and the political awakening of the county.

memes.label: Fanny Robarts
memes.description: Fanny Robarts, wife of Mark Robarts and intimate friend of Lady Lufton, whose steadfast loyalty and quiet strength anchor the domestic sphere of Framley Parsonage.

memes.label: Framley Court
memes.description: Framley Court, the country seat of the Lufton family in Barsetshire, the social and moral centre of the parish of Framley.

memes.label: Framley Parsonage
memes.description: The parsonage at Framley, residence of the vicar Mark Robarts and his family, provided by Lady Lufton's patronage.

memes.label: "前置内容"
memes.description: "Table of Contents and front matter for Framley Parsonage"

memes.label: Gentleman
memes.description: The Victorian ideal of the gentleman — a social and moral category central to the class structure and character judgments of Framley Parsonage.

memes.label: Her Majesty's Government
memes.description: Her Majesty's Government, the executive authority formed from the majority party in Parliament, directing the affairs of the nation in Framley Parsonage.

memes.label: Griselda Grantly
memes.description: Griselda Grantly, the beautiful daughter of Archdeacon Grantly and Mrs. Grantly, whose silent beauty and strategic marriage to Lord Dumbello form the centre of one of the novel's most carefully orchestrated courtships.

memes.label: Hogglestock
memes.description: Hogglestock, a poor rural parish in Barsetshire, whose impoverished curate embodies the hardships of the unreformed ecclesiastical system.

memes.label: The Jupiter
memes.description: The Jupiter — Trollope's fictionalized version of The Times — the powerful newspaper whose editorial voice shapes public opinion and political outcomes in Framley Parsonage.

memes.label: Lady Arabella Gresham
memes.description: Lady Arabella Gresham, the aristocratic mother of Frank Gresham and wife of Mr. Gresham of Greshamsbury, whose illness, social pride, and maternal anxieties render her one of the more complex background figures of the Barsetshire world.

memes.label: Lady Hartletop
memes.description: Lady Hartletop, the mother of Lord Dumbello, whose "meretricious grandeur" and London social standing make her a figure of worldly consequence — and a target of Trollope's sharpest satire on aristocratic pretension.

memes.label: Lady Lufton
memes.description: Lady Lufton of Framley Court, the formidable patroness whose social authority and moral certainty dominate the world of Framley Parsonage.

memes.label: Lady Meredith
memes.description: Lady Meredith, a friend of Fanny Robarts and a calm, sensible presence in the social world of Framley, whose steady counsel and mediating influence serve as a counterweight to the novel's more turbulent relationships.

memes.label: Lady Scatcherd
memes.description: Lady Scatcherd, a widow of Barsetshire whose pretty cottage and modest circumstances make her a figure of both sympathy and gentle comedy in the social world that surrounds Miss Dunstable.

memes.label: Ecclesiastical Living
memes.description: A Church of England parochial benefice — the endowed office providing a clergyman's income, house, and pastoral jurisdiction

memes.label: Lord Brock
memes.description: Lord Brock, the Whig Prime Minister during the events of the novel, whose distribution of ecclesiastical patronage drives significant plot developments and illuminates the political machinery of Trollope's Barsetshire.

memes.label: Lord De Terrier
memes.description: Lord De Terrier, the Tory leader recognized by his party as their standard-bearer, whose political fortunes and personal limitations illuminate the novel's anatomy of parliamentary leadership in the age of Lord Brock.

memes.label: Lord Dumbello
memes.description: Lord Dumbello, the taciturn eldest son of Lady Hartletop, whose mute courtship of Griselda Grantly provides one of Trollope's most economical satires of aristocratic manners.

memes.label: Lord Lufton
memes.description: Lord Lufton, son of Lady Lufton of Framley Court, whose romance with Lucy Robarts forms the central love story of Framley Parsonage.

memes.label: Lord Petty Bag
memes.description: Lord Petty Bag, a political office-holder whose position — with its "place of considerable emolument" — represents the spoils system of Victorian government patronage that Trollope satirises throughout the Barsetshire novels.

memes.label: Lucy Robarts
memes.description: Lucy Robarts, sister of Mark Robarts, whose quiet dignity, keen intelligence, and moral strength win the heart of Lord Lufton against his mother's opposition.

memes.label: Mark Robarts
memes.description: The Rev. Mark Robarts, vicar of Framley, young clergyman and protagonist whose financial entanglements drive the central plot of Framley Parsonage.

memes.label: Lord Lufton's Marriage to Lucy Robarts
memes.description: The marriage of Lord Lufton to Lucy Robarts — the romantic climax of Framley Parsonage, in which love triumphs over class prejudice and Lady Lufton's opposition.

memes.label: Miss Dunstable
memes.description: Miss Dunstable, the great heiress of the novel, whose immense fortune makes her the object of matrimonial ambition while her sharp wit and independence defy conventional expectations.

memes.label: Miss Grantly
memes.description: Miss Grantly (Griselda), the elder daughter of Archdeacon Grantly, whose great beauty and expected fortune make her a central figure in the matrimonial calculations of Barsetshire society.

memes.label: Miss Proudie
memes.description: Miss Proudie, the daughter of Bishop and Mrs. Proudie, whose appearance at Barchester social gatherings as a matrimonial candidate makes her both a pawn in her mother's ambitions and a sharp-eyed observer of the clerical society she is expected to ornament.

memes.label: Mr. Crawley
memes.description: Mr. Crawley, the impoverished perpetual curate of Hogglestock, whose austere integrity and grinding poverty make him one of the most compelling figures in the Barsetshire chronicles.

memes.label: Mr. Forrest
memes.description: Mr. Forrest, the manager of a bank in Barchester, who becomes Mark Robarts's confidential adviser in the matter of Sowerby's accommodation bill and proves an unexpected ally in Robarts's financial difficulties.

memes.label: Mr. Fothergill
memes.description: Mr. Fothergill, the Duke of Omnium's man of business at Gatherum Castle, whose role as intermediary between the great duke and the political world makes him a figure of quiet but substantial influence in Barsetshire society.

memes.label: Mr. Gresham
memes.description: Mr. Gresham of Greshamsbury, the richest commoner in the county, father of Frank Gresham, and a pillar of the conservative landed interest whose wealth and acres make him a figure of substantial social importance in Barsetshire.

memes.label: Mr. Harding
memes.description: Mr. Harding, the former Warden of Hiram's Hospital and now precentor of Barchester Cathedral, the gentle and beloved clergyman whose quiet sanctity provides the moral centre of Trollope's Barsetshire chronicles.

memes.label: Mr. Harold Smith
memes.description: Mr. Harold Smith, Member of Parliament and half of the formidable "Harold Smiths," a political couple whose social influence extends through the Chaldicotes set and into Barsetshire society.

memes.label: Mr. Slope
memes.description: Mr. Slope, the Bishop of Barchester's former chaplain whose evangelical ambition and romantic audacity made him one of the most controversial figures in the Barsetshire chronicles, now a spectral presence whose name still has the power to provoke.

memes.label: Mr. Smith
memes.description: Mr. Smith, a Member of Parliament and lecturer whose ponderous oration on the South Sea islands at Barchester provides some of the novel's most sustained comic relief.

memes.label: Mr. Supplehouse
memes.description: Mr. Supplehouse, a member of Parliament and a prominent figure in the Chaldicotes circle, whose political influence, caustic wit, and ambiguous reputation make him both a sought-after ally and a figure of suspicion in Barsetshire society.

memes.label: Mrs. Arabin
memes.description: Mrs. Arabin, the wife of the Dean of Barchester, whose benevolent interest in Lucy Robarts's welfare and whose position at the heart of the Barchester clerical establishment make her a quiet but significant figure in the novel's emotional and social landscape.

memes.label: Mrs. Crawley
memes.description: Mrs. Crawley, wife of the impoverished perpetual curate of Hogglestock, whose patient endurance of grinding poverty and unwavering support of her husband's austere integrity make her one of the novel's most affecting portraits of domestic virtue.

memes.label: Mrs. Grantly
memes.description: Mrs. Grantly, wife of the Archdeacon of Barchester, whose pride in family and acute social intelligence make her a key figure in the marriage politics that connect the Barsetshire novels.

memes.label: Mrs. Gresham
memes.description: Mrs. Gresham, niece of Dr. Thorne and wife of Mr. Gresham of Boxall Hill, whose moral sensibilities and earnest attempts to influence her uncle's views on marriage form a subtle portrait of domestic persuasion in the Barsetshire world.

memes.label: Mrs. Harold Smith
memes.description: Mrs. Harold Smith, a clever and socially adroit woman whose lively conversation and political connections make her a central figure in the Chaldicotes set that entangles Mark Robarts.

memes.label: Mrs. Proudie
memes.description: Mrs. Proudie, wife of the Bishop of Barchester, whose domineering personality and political ambition make her one of Trollope's most memorable portraits of ecclesiastical power exercised from the domestic sphere.

memes.label: Mrs. Smith
memes.description: Mrs. Smith, the innkeeper of Chaldicotes, who presides over the social gatherings of the Chaldicotes set with sharp wit and practical authority.

memes.label: Parish
memes.description: The parish as the fundamental unit of ecclesiastical organization in Framley Parsonage — a geographical, social, and spiritual jurisdiction.

memes.label: Parliament
memes.description: Parliament as a political institution in Framley Parsonage — the arena of national power that shapes the ambitions and anxieties of the novel's political characters.

memes.label: Patronage
memes.description: The system of granting offices, livings, and favors through personal or political influence in Victorian England

memes.label: Preferment
memes.description: Advancement to a higher ecclesiastical office or benefice in the Church of England — the mechanism of clerical career progression

memes.label: Sir George Meredith
memes.description: Sir George Meredith, a Framley landowner and the husband of Lady Meredith, whose presence in the neighbourhood shapes the social world of Framley Parsonage and whose marriage to Lady Meredith represents a triumph of maternal matchmaking.

memes.label: Mr. Sowerby
memes.description: Mr. Sowerby of Chaldicotes, a charming but financially reckless Whig MP whose schemes entangle Mark Robarts in debt and threaten the stability of Framley Parsonage.
